events.intro: Lifecycle events fire after every mutation. Callbacks are async-safe. Errors inside a callback are caught and logged without interrupting the HTTP response or service call.

types.fires: After a new document is inserted.
After any field on a document changes.
After a specific named field changes. One event per changed key.
When any element in an array field changes.
When the array element whose keyField === keyValue changes.
After a document is soft or hard deleted.
After a soft-deleted document is restored.

events.nullability: TypeScript resolves callback argument nullability from the event string at compile time. update and update.* callbacks receive non-null Partial<T> for both whatWas and whatIs. create and restore callbacks receive null for both. delete callbacks receive non-null whatWas and null for whatIs.

examples.label: Send email on status change
Watch a specific token in extra_data
Rebuild search index on create or update
Audit log on delete

interceptors.intro: Interceptors are standard Express (req, res, next) middleware functions mounted per action type. They run before the controller handler and can block, modify, or pass through the request.
notes: Multiple interceptors for the same action run in registration order.
The all action type matches every request regardless of method.
Each interceptor must call next() to continue or send a response to halt.

examples.label: RBAC on delete
Scope reads to the current user
Inject updated_by on every write
Request logging

nested.intro: Nested operations target nested objects and arrays using MongoDB operators directly, bypassing the shallow Object.assign merge used by the standard update method. Every object inside an array receives an auto-injected _mmid (a stable UUID) that gives each item an addressable identity without requiring a sub-schema.
nested.mmid: _mmid is injected automatically on save and on document init. It persists across updates. Use it as the stable identity key for patch_item and pull_id instead of array index, which shifts when items are added or removed.

operations.name: push_many
operations.value: An array of items to append.

operations.name: pull
operations.value: A filter object. Items matching the filter are removed.

operations.name: pull_id
operations.value: The _mmid string of the item to remove.

operations.name: add_to_set
operations.value: A single value. Appended only if not already present.

operations.name: increment
operations.value: A number. Use negative values to decrement.

relations.intro: Entities declare relations to other entities at initialisation time. Relations enforce foreign keys at the schema level and enable automatic population at query time.

types.name: parent
types.summary: Declares that this entity belongs to another. A required foreign key field is injected into the schema.

types.name: children
types.summary: Declares which entities are children of this one. Child results are populated on withChildren calls as paginated sub-collections.

types.name: sister
types.summary: Declares a peer relation. A required foreign key is injected. Sister entities do not nest inside each other but their FK constraint ensures data consistency.

validation.intro: Validation uses Joi schemas defined at entity initialisation. Reserved fields (uuid, slug, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) are stripped automatically and never validated from user input.
validation.createSchema: Joi schema map for fields accepted on create. Required constraints are enforced.
validation.updateSchema: Joi schema map for fields accepted on update. If omitted, defaults to createSchema with all keys set to optional automatically.
validation.options: createValidationOptions and updateValidationOptions accept any Joi.ValidationOptions. The default is { abortEarly: false, allowUnknown: true }.
validation.skipValidation: Pass skipValidation: true to CreateOptions or UpdateOptions to bypass Joi entirely for that call. Useful for seeding or internal migrations.
validation.programmatic: Call entity.service.validate(data, 'create') or entity.service.validate(data, 'update') to run validation without inserting. Returns { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }.

typescript.intro: The package ships complete .d.ts declarations. All generics are optional - the package works in plain JavaScript without any type annotations.
typescript.extending: Extend BaseEntityDocument to type your additional fields. Pass the interface as the generic to MetaEntity.
typescript.eventPaths: EventPath<T> produces a union of all valid event strings including typed dot-paths under update.*. TypeScript's IDE autocomplete surfaces these as suggestions.
typescript.callbackTypes: CallbackForEvent<T, E> resolves the correct callback signature for a given event string E. update.* callbacks receive non-null Partial<T> for both whatWas and whatIs. create and restore receive null. delete receives non-null whatWas and null whatIs.
